goal: slop.language.ast: introduce Node StrEnum as single source of truth for tree-sitter node-type strings; deduplicate across all 11 grammar files' frozenset returns and extract_name comparisons

summary.title: Node StrEnum for tree-sitter type strings
summary.what: Added slop.language.ast.Node — a flat StrEnum (~100 members) covering every tree-sitter node-type string referenced by v2 grammars. Refactored all 11 grammar files to import and reference enum members instead of inline string literals. Affects every classmethod that returns a frozenset of node types (callable, classes, methods, functions, identifiers, decision_nodes, nesting_nodes, compensating_decisions, definition_unwrap_types, boolean_op_node) plus node.type comparisons inside the C/C++/Ruby/Julia extract_name overrides. Behavior identical: 486 tests pass.
summary.why: Same tree-sitter type strings ('if_statement', 'function_definition', 'binary_expression', etc.) appeared verbatim across 9+ grammar files. Risk of typos was real and silent — a misspelled 'if_statment' would yield CCX=1 with no parse error. Pulling the strings into a single StrEnum gives IDE autocomplete, typo-proofing, cross-grammar grep visibility, and a documented inventory of every tree-sitter node type the v2 grammars depend on.
summary.user_goal: slop.language.ast: introduce Node StrEnum as single source of truth for tree-sitter node-type strings; deduplicate across all 11 grammar files' frozenset returns and extract_name comparisons

decisions.chose: One flat StrEnum (Node) covering all node types, grouped by category in source order via comments only.
decisions.rationale: Language-namespace enums (PythonNode, JsNode, ...) would explode complexity and defeat the dedup goal — many strings (if_statement, function_definition, identifier) appear in 6+ grammars. A flat enum is the right tradeoff between centrality and simplicity.

decisions.chose: StrEnum (Python 3.11+) rather than a regular Enum with .value access.
decisions.rationale: StrEnum members ARE strings — `frozenset({Node.IF_STATEMENT})` is set-equal to `frozenset({'if_statement'})`. The walker compares `node.type` (a raw tree-sitter string) against the frozensets without conversion. Switching to regular Enum would require .value lookups everywhere.

decisions.chose: Member name = uppercase of the tree-sitter type string verbatim (IF_STATEMENT = 'if_statement', not IF or IfStatement).
decisions.rationale: Removes naming ambiguity (Ruby has 'if' as a node type AND many languages have 'if_statement'; uppercasing each verbatim disambiguates: Node.IF vs Node.IF_STATEMENT). Matches the tree-sitter docs reader's mental model.

decisions.chose: Place under slop/language/ast/ as a sub-package, even though only nodes.py exists today.
decisions.rationale: User-directed location. The package shape anticipates future ast-related additions (field-name registry, traversal helpers) without restructuring. slop/_ast/ is already taken at the top level for tree-sitter loaders.

decisions.chose: Exclude tree-sitter field names, operator-text strings, and punctuation tokens from the enum.
decisions.rationale: Field names ('body', 'name', 'declarator') live in a different namespace (tree-sitter field naming on grammar definitions). Operator-text strings ('&&', '||', 'and', 'or') are source-code character sequences, not type names. Punctuation tokens ('def', 'self', '.', ',') are syntactic. Including them would conflate three different categories under one name.

review_notes: 486 tests pass — identical to pre-refactor count. Refactor is pure mechanical: same strings, just behind enum members.
Categories used as source-order comments only (no programmatic grouping). Future cognitive port may add 'nesting' as a typed namespace within Node if the surface grows past comfortable flat-listing.
Pyright cannot resolve slop.language.ast imports (IDE config issue with the venv-less workspace); imports work fine at runtime, confirmed by full test pass.

architectural_claims: Tree-sitter node-type strings consumed by v2 grammars live in slop.language.ast.Node, a flat StrEnum. No grammar should reference an inline string literal for a tree-sitter node type going forward.

Field names, operator-text strings, and punctuation tokens are explicitly NOT in Node — they live in different namespaces (field names: tree-sitter grammar-side; operators: source character sequences; punctuation: syntactic tokens).
migration_notes: Future grammar additions: import `from ..ast import Node` and use enum members. Adding a new tree-sitter node type means adding one line to slop/language/ast/nodes.py and referencing the member.
